input.description:
swissarmyhammer-kanban-app/src/commands.rs:477-507

The `cmd` parameter comes directly from the frontend as an untrusted string. While there is no injection risk (it is used as a HashMap key lookup), there is no validation that the command ID matches the expected dotted format (e.g. 'task.add'). Arbitrary strings like empty strings or very long strings are accepted and simply produce 'Unknown command' errors.

This is defense-in-depth -- the current behavior is safe (HashMap::get returns None), but adding a quick format check would produce better error messages and prevent accidental misuse.

Suggestion: Consider a lightweight validation like checking for non-empty, reasonable length, and ASCII-only characters. Low priority since there is no actual vulnerability.
input.title: dispatch_command does not validate command ID format before registry lookup

input.description:
swissarmyhammer-kanban/tests/command_dispatch_integration.rs

The 13 integration tests cover add, move, untag, update_field, availability, UI state, and a full session. However, there is no test for the undo/redo path via `app.undo` / `app.redo` commands. These commands require a transaction ID arg, and there are no tests verifying:
1. That the operation_id returned from a mutation can be passed to app.undo
2. That app.undo actually reverses the mutation
3. That app.redo re-applies it

This is the most complex data flow in the dispatch system and is currently untested at the integration level.

Suggestion: Add an integration test that does task.add -> captures operation_id -> dispatches app.undo with that id -> verifies the task is gone -> dispatches app.redo -> verifies the task is back.
input.title: No integration test for undo/redo via dispatch_command

input.description: board-view.tsx defines `handleDragEnd` (line 265) with `persistMove` in its useCallback dependency array (line 353), but `persistMove` is a `const` defined later (line 382). Since `const` is not hoisted like function declarations, accessing it before initialization triggers a ReferenceError (temporal dead zone). Move `persistMove` above `handleDragEnd` to fix. This is a regression from converting the plain `async function persistMove()` (which was hoisted) to `const persistMove = useCallback(...)` (which is not).

input.description: entity-focus-context.tsx: `useFocusedScope()` calls `getScope(focusedMoniker)` which reads from a `useRef` (registryRef). Since ref mutations don't trigger re-renders, there is a race: if a FocusScope mounts and registers its scope *after* `setFocus` is called for that moniker, `useFocusedScope` will return null (stale) because the component never re-rendered after the ref write. In practice this may not manifest often because `setFocus` updates state (causing re-render) and by that time the scope is usually registered. But the timing is fragile -- if a component calls `setFocus` in the same effect as a FocusScope mounts, the scope may not be in the registry yet. Consider documenting this ordering constraint or adding a safety re-read.
input.title: WARNING: useFocusedScope reads from ref without triggering re-render

output.description: entity-focus-context.tsx: `useFocusedScope()` calls `getScope(focusedMoniker)` which reads from a `useRef` (registryRef). Since ref mutations don't trigger re-renders, there is a race: if a FocusScope mounts and registers its scope *after* `setFocus` is called for that moniker, `useFocusedScope` will return null (stale) because the component never re-rendered after the ref write. In practice this may not manifest often because `setFocus` updates state (causing re-render) and by that time the scope is usually registered. But the timing is fragile -- if a component calls `setFocus` in the same effect as a FocusScope mounts, the scope may not be in the registry yet. Consider documenting this ordering constraint or adding a safety re-read.
output.title: WARNING: useFocusedScope reads from ref without triggering re-render

input.description: command-scope.tsx: `resolveCommand()` looks up by `id` alone (line 106: `current.commands.get(id)`), but `collectAvailableCommands()` uses target-aware shadow keys (`id + ':' + (target ?? '')`). This means `resolveCommand('entity.inspect')` returns the first match in the chain regardless of target, while `collectAvailableCommands` would accumulate multiple entity.inspect commands with different targets. The KeybindingHandler in app-shell.tsx uses `resolveCommand` for focused-scope dispatch, meaning keyboard shortcuts for `entity.inspect` will always hit the innermost scope's version, even when multiple target-differentiated inspect commands exist. This may be intentional (keyboard shortcut acts on nearest entity) but should be documented since it differs from the context menu behavior.

output.description: board-view.tsx defines `handleDragEnd` (line 265) with `persistMove` in its useCallback dependency array (line 353), but `persistMove` is a `const` defined later (line 382). Since `const` is not hoisted like function declarations, accessing it before initialization triggers a ReferenceError (temporal dead zone). Move `persistMove` above `handleDragEnd` to fix. This is a regression from converting the plain `async function persistMove()` (which was hoisted) to `const persistMove = useCallback(...)` (which is not). #review-finding

output.description:
swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/commands/entity_commands.rs:47-49

DeleteEntityCmd::available() checks `ctx.target.is_some()` but the YAML definition in entity.yaml defines:
```yaml
params:
  - name: moniker
    from: args
```
This means the frontend may pass the moniker as an arg rather than as the target field. The availability check on `ctx.target` would return false when the moniker is in args instead. Either the available() check should also look at args['moniker'], or the YAML param should be `from: target`.

The execute() method also reads from `ctx.target` (line 54-57), which is consistent with available() but inconsistent with the YAML.

Suggestion: Change the YAML param to `from: target` to match the implementation, or update available()/execute() to also check `ctx.arg('moniker')`. #review-finding #warning
output.title: entity.delete available() checks target field but YAML uses moniker arg

output.description:
swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/commands/ui_commands.rs:26-28

InspectCmd::execute() falls back to `ctx.scope_chain.first()` when no target is set. The scope chain can contain any moniker type (column, board, etc.), and the first entry is the innermost scope. This means the inspect command could receive a moniker like 'board:board' and push it onto the inspector stack even though it may not be meaningful to inspect.

The available() check (line 14-15) allows the command when the scope chain is non-empty, regardless of what types are in it.

Suggestion: Consider filtering scope_chain entries to inspectable types, or at minimum document this is intentional. #review-finding #warning
output.title: ui.inspect falls back to first scope_chain entry without checking if it's inspectable

output.description:
swissarmyhammer-kanban-app/src/commands.rs:570-588

Inside the `available.retain()` closure, a new CommandContext is constructed for each command definition. This clones the scope chain and creates a new empty HashMap on each iteration. For a registry with ~21 commands, this is not a real performance concern, but it is worth noting as the registry grows.

Additionally, `active_handle` is awaited once and then the Arc is cloned via `ref handle` inside the closure, which is correct.

Suggestion: Hoist the args HashMap and build a single reusable context template that only swaps the command_id. Low priority given current scale. #review-finding #warning
output.title: list_available_commands clones scope and creates HashMap on every retain iteration

output.description:
swissarmyhammer-kanban-app/src/commands.rs:477-507

The `cmd` parameter comes directly from the frontend as an untrusted string. While there is no injection risk (it is used as a HashMap key lookup), there is no validation that the command ID matches the expected dotted format (e.g. 'task.add'). Arbitrary strings like empty strings or very long strings are accepted and simply produce 'Unknown command' errors.

This is defense-in-depth -- the current behavior is safe (HashMap::get returns None), but adding a quick format check would produce better error messages and prevent accidental misuse.

Suggestion: Consider a lightweight validation like checking for non-empty, reasonable length, and ASCII-only characters. Low priority since there is no actual vulnerability. #review-finding #warning
output.title: dispatch_command does not validate command ID format before registry lookup

output.description:
swissarmyhammer-kanban/tests/command_dispatch_integration.rs

The 13 integration tests cover add, move, untag, update_field, availability, UI state, and a full session. However, there is no test for the undo/redo path via `app.undo` / `app.redo` commands. These commands require a transaction ID arg, and there are no tests verifying:
1. That the operation_id returned from a mutation can be passed to app.undo
2. That app.undo actually reverses the mutation
3. That app.redo re-applies it

This is the most complex data flow in the dispatch system and is currently untested at the integration level.

Suggestion: Add an integration test that does task.add -> captures operation_id -> dispatches app.undo with that id -> verifies the task is gone -> dispatches app.redo -> verifies the task is back. #review-finding #warning
output.title: No integration test for undo/redo via dispatch_command
RwLock on commands_registry held across an await point in dispatch_command

input.description:
Launch the Tauri app and verify the full data flow: initial load, mutation via commands, and event-driven refresh.

## Data Reload Architecture

```
User action (palette, keybinding, context menu, drag-drop)
  → dispatchCommand(cmd) in command-scope.tsx
    → invoke("dispatch_command", { cmd, target, args }) to Rust
      → commands.rs: looks up Command impl, builds CommandContext, executes
      → if undoable: app.emit("board-changed", ())
  → App.tsx listens for "board-changed" event
    → calls refresh() which does parallel fetches:
        get_board_data  → board, columns, swimlanes, tags (with counts)
        list_open_boards → sidebar board list
        list_entities(task) → all tasks (with enrichment)
    → sets React state: setBoard, setTaskEntities, setTagEntities
    → React re-renders board view
```

## Key event sources
- `dispatch_command` (commands.rs:558-561): emits `board-changed` for undoable commands
- `handle_menu_event` (menu.rs:261): emits `board-changed` for native menu actions  
- `field-update-context.tsx`: field edits dispatch `entity.update_field` → Rust emits event
- Drag-drop in `board-view.tsx`: dispatches `task.move` → Rust emits event

## Checklist

- [x] Board name visible in nav
- [ ] Columns render (todo, doing, done)
- [ ] Tasks visible in columns
- [ ] Add task via palette → task appears without manual refresh
- [ ] Edit task title in inspector → title updates on board
- [ ] Drag task between columns → position persists after reload
- [ ] Delete task → card disappears
- [ ] Switch keymap in palette → Settings menu radio updates
- [ ] Escape closes inspector panel
- [ ] Escape closes palette (vim: normal-mode Escape closes)
- [ ] Check browser console for [keybindings], [dispatch] debug logs
- [ ] Check Rust logs for dispatch_command tracing output
- [ ] No console errors

#test #sample
input.title: UI smoke test: verify board loads and data reload works

- [ ] `cell-number.tsx` — right-aligned number

- [ ] Props: `entities: Entity[]`, `fields: FieldDef[]`, `cursor: {row, col}`, `selection: Set<number>`, `mode`
- [ ] Sticky `<thead>` with column headers from field names
- [ ] `<tbody>` rows from entities, each cell rendered via CellDispatch
- [ ] Focused cell (matching cursor) gets ring/border highlight
- [ ] Selected rows get background highlight
- [ ] Column widths from `field.width` or sensible defaults
- [ ] Empty state: "No tasks" message when entities is empty

**Update `ui/src/components/grid-view.tsx`:**
- [ ] Use `useSchema()` to get field definitions for entityType
- [ ] Use `useEntityStore()` to get entities
- [ ] Filter fieldNames through schema to get ordered FieldDef[]
- [ ] Initialize `useGrid({rowCount: entities.length, colCount: fields.length})`
- [ ] Render `<DataTable>` with all props wired up
- [ ] Table renders correctly with task data from entity store
output.title: DataTable renderer — static grid

- [ ] Connect execute functions: grid.up→moveUp, grid.down→moveDown, grid.left→moveLeft, grid.right→moveRight, grid.top→moveToTop, grid.bottom→moveToBottom, grid.firstCol→moveToFirstCol, grid.lastCol→moveToLastCol, grid.edit→enterEdit, grid.selectToggle→toggleSelection, grid.selectDown→selectDown, grid.selectUp→selectUp
- [ ] Grid container div: `tabIndex={0}`, auto-focus on mount
- [ ] Grid commands appear in command palette when grid is focused
- [ ] Commands pass through to parent scope when not handled (e.g., `:view board`)

- [ ] Arrow keys (cua) navigate cells

input.description:
Implement cell editing flow: grid.edit (i/Enter) mounts editor in focused cell, commit persists value, returns to normal mode.

**Create cell editor components in `ui/src/components/cells/`:**
- [ ] `cell-editor-markdown.tsx` — CM6 inline editor, reuse pattern from FieldPlaceholderEditor. Single/multi-line from field def. Vim/CUA/Emacs keymaps from useKeymap().
- [ ] `cell-editor-select.tsx` — Radix Popover/DropdownMenu for select fields. Shows options, single click commits.
- [ ] `cell-editor-multi-select.tsx` — Checkbox list popover for multi-select. Toggle options, Enter/Escape commits.
- [ ] `cell-editor-date.tsx` — Native `<input type="date">` initially.
- [ ] `cell-editor-color.tsx` — Reuse ColorField pattern from entity-inspector.
- [ ] `cell-editor-number.tsx` — Numeric input with min/max from field def.
- [ ] `cell-editor-dispatch.tsx` — Routes editor type to correct component. Computed fields (editor=none) not editable.

**Update `ui/src/components/data-table.tsx`:**
- [ ] When `mode === "edit"` and cell matches cursor, render editor instead of display
- [ ] Editor receives `value`, `field`, `onCommit`, `onCancel` props

**Update `ui/src/components/grid-view.tsx`:**
- [ ] `onCellCommit` callback persists via `useFieldUpdate().updateField()`
- [ ] After commit/cancel, `exitEdit()` returns grid to normal mode

- [ ] i/Enter opens appropriate editor
- [ ] Escape exits edit mode
- [ ] Value persisted via updateField Tauri command
- [ ] Computed fields skip editing
input.title: Cell editors + edit mode

input.description:
Implement grid.delete, grid.newBelow, grid.newAbove, grid.copy, grid.paste commands. These compose existing Tauri backend entity primitives.

**Update `ui/src/hooks/use-grid.ts`:**
- [ ] Add `clipboard: Record<string, unknown> | null` to state
- [ ] Add `setCopyBuffer()` and `getCopyBuffer()` methods

**Update `ui/src/components/grid-view.tsx` commands:**
- [ ] `grid.delete` (dd / Delete) — delete entity at cursor row (or all selected rows). Dispatch via `invoke("dispatch_command", {cmd: "task.delete", ...})`. Confirm if multiple selected.
- [ ] `grid.newBelow` (o) — create new entity below cursor via `task.add`. Cursor moves to new row.
- [ ] `grid.newAbove` (O) — create new entity above cursor.
- [ ] `grid.copy` (yy / Mod+C) — copy focused row field values to clipboard buffer.
- [ ] `grid.paste` (p / Mod+V) — create new entity from clipboard buffer values.

- [ ] Cursor adjusts correctly after insert/delete (no out-of-bounds)
- [ ] Entity store updates reactively via existing Tauri event listeners in App.tsx
- [ ] Bulk delete works with visual selection
input.title: Row CRUD commands + copy/paste

output.description:
Create reusable cell display components for rendering field values in read-only table cells. Extract patterns from existing FieldDispatch in entity-inspector.tsx.

**Create `ui/src/components/cells/` directory:**
- [ ] `cell-text.tsx` — plain text display (text/markdown display types)
- [ ] `cell-badge.tsx` — single colored badge for select fields, using SelectOption colors from field def
- [ ] `cell-badge-list.tsx` — horizontal badge row for multi-select/computed tags, overflow hidden
- [ ] `cell-date.tsx` — formatted date display
- [ ] `cell-color-swatch.tsx` — small color circle
- [ ] `cell-number.tsx` — right-aligned number
- [ ] `cell-dispatch.tsx` — routes display type to correct component, falls back to cell-text

**Props pattern:** Each takes `{value: unknown, field: FieldDef}` and renders compact single-line cell.

**Key files to reference:**
- `entity-inspector.tsx` — existing FieldDispatch pattern
- `entity-card.tsx` — existing field rendering in cards
- Reuse existing `Badge` shadcn component

- [ ] All 7 display types handled (text, markdown, badge, badge-list, date, color-swatch, number)
- [ ] Null/undefined values handled gracefully
- [ ] Components are compact (single-line height, truncated overflow)
output.title: Cell display components

Add Grid variant to backend ViewKind enum, create builtin tasks-grid.yaml, and wire frontend routing so clicking the grid icon renders a skeleton GridView component.

**Backend (Rust):**
- [ ] Add `Grid` to `ViewKind` enum in `swissarmyhammer-views/src/types.rs`
- [ ] Create `swissarmyhammer-kanban/builtin/views/tasks-grid.yaml` — id: `01JMVIEW0000000000TGRID`, name: `Tasks`, icon: `table-2`, kind: `grid`, entity_type: `task`, card_fields: `[title, tags, assignees, due, depends_on]`
- [ ] Update any test asserting builtin view count (currently checks board only)
- [ ] `cargo test` passes

output.description:
Implement cell editing flow: grid.edit (i/Enter) mounts editor in focused cell, commit persists value, returns to normal mode.

**Create cell editor components in `ui/src/components/cells/`:**
- [ ] `cell-editor-markdown.tsx` — CM6 inline editor, reuse pattern from FieldPlaceholderEditor. Single/multi-line from field def. Vim/CUA/Emacs keymaps from useKeymap().
- [ ] `cell-editor-select.tsx` — Radix Popover/DropdownMenu for select fields. Shows options, single click commits.
- [ ] `cell-editor-multi-select.tsx` — Checkbox list popover for multi-select. Toggle options, Enter/Escape commits.
- [ ] `cell-editor-date.tsx` — Native `<input type="date">` initially.
- [ ] `cell-editor-color.tsx` — Reuse ColorField pattern from entity-inspector.
- [ ] `cell-editor-number.tsx` — Numeric input with min/max from field def.
- [ ] `cell-editor-dispatch.tsx` — Routes editor type to correct component. Computed fields (editor=none) not editable.

**Update `ui/src/components/data-table.tsx`:**
- [ ] When `mode === "edit"` and cell matches cursor, render editor instead of display
- [ ] Editor receives `value`, `field`, `onCommit`, `onCancel` props

**Update `ui/src/components/grid-view.tsx`:**
- [ ] `onCellCommit` callback persists via `useFieldUpdate().updateField()`
- [ ] After commit/cancel, `exitEdit()` returns grid to normal mode

- [ ] i/Enter opens appropriate editor
- [ ] Escape exits edit mode
- [ ] Value persisted via updateField Tauri command
- [ ] Computed fields skip editing
output.title: Cell editors + edit mode

output.description:
Implement grid.delete, grid.newBelow, grid.newAbove, grid.copy, grid.paste commands. These compose existing Tauri backend entity primitives.

**Update `ui/src/hooks/use-grid.ts`:**
- [ ] Add `clipboard: Record<string, unknown> | null` to state
- [ ] Add `setCopyBuffer()` and `getCopyBuffer()` methods

**Update `ui/src/components/grid-view.tsx` commands:**
- [ ] `grid.delete` (dd / Delete) — delete entity at cursor row (or all selected rows). Dispatch via `invoke("dispatch_command", {cmd: "task.delete", ...})`. Confirm if multiple selected.
- [ ] `grid.newBelow` (o) — create new entity below cursor via `task.add`. Cursor moves to new row.
- [ ] `grid.newAbove` (O) — create new entity above cursor.
- [ ] `grid.copy` (yy / Mod+C) — copy focused row field values to clipboard buffer.
- [ ] `grid.paste` (p / Mod+V) — create new entity from clipboard buffer values.

- [ ] Cursor adjusts correctly after insert/delete (no out-of-bounds)
- [ ] Entity store updates reactively via existing Tauri event listeners in App.tsx
- [ ] Bulk delete works with visual selection
output.title: Row CRUD commands + copy/paste

input.description:
Create `ui/src/components/fields/field-display.tsx` — a single dispatcher that routes on `field.display` (from YAML config) with a `mode` parameter (`compact` for grid, `full` for inspector).

Dispatches:
- `text` → TextDisplay (truncated in compact, full in full)
- `markdown` → MarkdownDisplay (truncated in compact, ReactMarkdown+GFM in full)
- `badge` → BadgeDisplay (colored badge from SelectOption)
- `badge-list` → BadgeListDisplay (TagPill list for tags, badge list for references)
- `number` → NumberDisplay (right-aligned tabular-nums)
- `date` → DateDisplay (formatted date)
- `color-swatch` → ColorSwatchDisplay (circle + hex in compact, larger in full)
- `avatar` → AvatarDisplay (placeholder for now)

Interface:
```tsx
interface FieldDisplayProps {
  field: FieldDef;
  value: unknown;
  entity: Entity;
  mode: "compact" | "full";
}
export function FieldDisplay({ field, value, entity, mode }: FieldDisplayProps)
```

- [ ] Create field-display.tsx with FieldDisplay dispatcher
- [ ] Move existing cell display components (TextCell, BadgeCell, etc.) into this file or import them, adding mode parameter
- [ ] Refactor CellDispatch to delegate to FieldDisplay with mode="compact"
- [ ] Refactor inspector FieldDispatch read-only paths to delegate to FieldDisplay with mode="full"
- [ ] Handle special cases: computed fields with derive (progress bar), body field tag decorations
- [ ] Run tests
input.title: Unified FieldDisplay dispatcher

input.description:
Replace hand-rolled sorting in DataTable with TanStack Table (`@tanstack/react-table`). Add grouping support.

Current state:
- `data-table.tsx` has custom `compareValues()` and `sortedRows` useMemo for sorting
- No grouping capability
- `useGrid` manages cursor/mode/selection — this stays as-is (vim layer on top)

Integration approach:
- Use `useReactTable()` from TanStack with `getCoreRowModel`, `getSortedRowModel`, `getGroupedRowModel`
- Map `DataTableColumn[]` to TanStack `ColumnDef[]` with `accessorFn: (row) => row.fields[field.name]`
- Custom cell renderer via TanStack's `cell` column option — calls shared display components
- TanStack handles sort state and grouping state; remove custom `compareValues()` and `sortedRows`
- Keep `useGrid` cursor navigation — it operates on row indices from TanStack's `getRowModel().rows`
- Header click toggles TanStack sorting (replaces custom `handleHeaderClick`)
- Add column grouping via view YAML config (`group_by` field?)

Files to modify:
- `data-table.tsx` — replace sort logic with TanStack, add grouping
- `grid-view.tsx` — pass TanStack table instance, adapt useGrid rowCount to flattened row model

Files to add:
- None — TanStack is used inline in DataTable

- [ ] Install `@tanstack/react-table`
- [ ] Refactor DataTable to use `useReactTable()` with sorted and grouped row models
- [ ] Remove custom `compareValues()` and sort state from DataTable
- [ ] Map DataTableColumn to TanStack ColumnDef with existing cell display components
- [ ] Add grouping support (group by column via header menu or config)
- [ ] Ensure useGrid cursor navigation still works over TanStack's row model
- [ ] Ensure inline editing still works (renderEditor for cursor cell)
- [ ] Run tests

input.description: In data-table.tsx, `flatRows` from TanStack includes both group header rows and data rows. The `ri` index from `flatRows.map((row, ri) => ...)` is used for `grid.cursor.row` comparison, but group header rows increment `ri` without being real data rows. This means cursor highlighting (`ri === grid.cursor.row`) and `grid.setCursor(ri, ...)` will be off when grouping is active -- the grid hook tracks data-row indices but `ri` includes group rows.\n\nThis is latent because grouping is not yet externally enabled, but will break as soon as `groupingProp` is used.
input.title: data-table: cursor row index ri counts group header rows

output.description: In data-table.tsx, `flatRows` from TanStack includes both group header rows and data rows. The `ri` index from `flatRows.map((row, ri) => ...)` is used for `grid.cursor.row` comparison, but group header rows increment `ri` without being real data rows. This means cursor highlighting (`ri === grid.cursor.row`) and `grid.setCursor(ri, ...)` will be off when grouping is active -- the grid hook tracks data-row indices but `ri` includes group rows.\n\nThis is latent because grouping is not yet externally enabled, but will break as soon as `groupingProp` is used.
output.title: data-table: cursor row index ri counts group header rows

Currently the inspector edit path bypasses the shared editor abstraction for its most common case.\n\nThis is a partial deduplication -- the whole point of card 2 was to funnel through shared editors.

- `data-table.tsx` — replace sort logic with TanStack, add grouping

output.description: In entity-inspector.tsx lines 252-260, the editing dispatch for the default/markdown case uses FieldPlaceholderEditor directly rather than the shared MarkdownEditor component with mode='full'. The MarkdownEditor was designed for exactly this purpose and would use EditableMarkdown in full mode. Currently the inspector edit path bypasses the shared editor abstraction for its most common case.\n\nThis is a partial deduplication -- the whole point of card 2 was to funnel through shared editors.
output.title: entity-inspector: FieldDispatch edit path for default markdown uses FieldPlaceholderEditor instead of MarkdownEditor

output.description: In markdown-display.tsx, `forwardRef` is imported on line 55, after the MarkdownDisplay function definition. While this compiles (hoisting), it breaks the convention of all imports at the top of the file and will confuse linters/readers. Move to the top import block.
output.title: markdown-display: forwardRef import after component definition

output.description: In data-table.tsx, `flatRows` from TanStack includes both group header rows and data rows. The `ri` index from `flatRows.map((row, ri) => ...)` is used for `grid.cursor.row` comparison, but group header rows increment `ri` without being real data rows. This means cursor highlighting (`ri === grid.cursor.row`) and `grid.setCursor(ri, ...)` will be off when grouping is active -- the grid hook tracks data-row indices but `ri` includes group rows.\n\nThis is latent because grouping is not yet externally enabled, but will break as soon as `groupingProp` is used.
output.title: data-table: cursor row index ri counts group header rows

input.value: UI smoke test: verify board loads and data reload works!

output.body:
Launch the Tauri app and verify the full data flow: initial load, mutation via commands, and event-driven refresh.

## Data Reload Architecture

```
User action (palette, keybinding, context menu, drag-drop)
  → dispatchCommand(cmd) in command-scope.tsx
    → invoke("dispatch_command", { cmd, target, args }) to Rust
      → commands.rs: looks up Command impl, builds CommandContext, executes
      → if undoable: app.emit("board-changed", ())
  → App.tsx listens for "board-changed" event
    → calls refresh() which does parallel fetches:
        get_board_data  → board, columns, swimlanes, tags (with counts)
        list_open_boards → sidebar board list
        list_entities(task) → all tasks (with enrichment)
    → sets React state: setBoard, setTaskEntities, setTagEntities
    → React re-renders board view
```

## Key event sources
- `dispatch_command` (commands.rs:558-561): emits `board-changed` for undoable commands
- `handle_menu_event` (menu.rs:261): emits `board-changed` for native menu actions  
- `field-update-context.tsx`: field edits dispatch `entity.update_field` → Rust emits event
- Drag-drop in `board-view.tsx`: dispatches `task.move` → Rust emits event

## Checklist

- [x] Board name visible in nav
- [ ] Columns render (todo, doing, done)
- [ ] Tasks visible in columns
- [ ] Add task via palette → task appears without manual refresh
- [ ] Edit task title in inspector → title updates on board
- [ ] Drag task between columns → position persists after reload
- [ ] Delete task → card disappears
- [ ] Switch keymap in palette → Settings menu radio updates
- [ ] Escape closes inspector panel
- [ ] Escape closes palette (vim: normal-mode Escape closes)
- [ ] Check browser console for [keybindings], [dispatch] debug logs
- [ ] Check Rust logs for dispatch_command tracing output
- [ ] No console errors

#test #sample #smoke-test #reload
output.title: UI smoke test: verify board loads and data reload works!

input.description:
Replace the native `<input type="date">` with a consistent date editor used in both grid and inspector.

## Design
- Grid cell: renders `DateDisplay` as popover trigger (no layout jump, same as color pattern)
- Inspector: same component
- Popover contains:
  - CM6 text input for natural language ("tomorrow", "next friday", etc.)
  - Live preview showing resolved YYYY-MM-DD
  - Shadcn Calendar (react-day-picker) below for click-to-pick
  - Clicking a calendar day fills the CM6 input
  - On commit, saves resolved YYYY-MM-DD (not the NL text)

## Subtasks
- [ ] Install dependencies: `chrono-node`, shadcn calendar (`react-day-picker`)
- [ ] Create shadcn Calendar UI component
- [ ] Rewrite `DateEditor` with CM6 input + chrono-node parsing + calendar picker in popover
- [ ] Grid cell: use `DateDisplay` as trigger, popover floats over (no p-0 jump)
- [ ] Inspector: same DateEditor component via FieldDispatch
- [ ] Verify both grid and inspector use identical picker
input.title: Natural language date editor with CM6 + calendar picker

input.description:
Use `whoami` crate to automatically create an actor for the logged-in OS user when a board is opened.

## Changes
- `swissarmyhammer-kanban-app/Cargo.toml` — add `whoami` dependency
- `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/actor/add.rs` — extend `AddActor` to accept optional `color` and `avatar` fields
- `swissarmyhammer-kanban-app/src/state.rs` — add `ensure_os_actor()` called after `BoardHandle::open()`

## Design
- Actor ID = `whoami::username()`, display name = `whoami::realname()`
- Derive deterministic hex color from username hash (mod a curated palette)
- Generate initials-based SVG avatar as data URI
- Try to read macOS profile picture from filesystem, prefer real photo over generated
- Use `AddActor::human(username, realname).with_ensure()` — idempotent on re-open

## Subtasks
- [ ] Add `whoami` to Cargo.toml
- [ ] Extend AddActor with optional color/avatar fields
- [ ] Implement `ensure_os_actor()` in state.rs
- [ ] Generate deterministic color from username hash
- [ ] Generate initials SVG avatar (or read macOS profile picture)
- [ ] Call ensure_os_actor after BoardHandle::open()
- [ ] Run `cargo test`
input.title: Auto-create OS user actor on board open

When an MCP client connects, auto-create an agent actor from the client info.

## Changes
- `swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/server.rs` — in `initialize()` handler (~line 920), after file watching setup, create actor

## Design
- Actor ID = slugified `request.client_info.name` (e.g. "claude-code")
- Display name = `request.client_info.name` (or `title` if present)
- `actor_type: agent`, `ensure: true`
- Derive deterministic color from name hash
- Generate geometric/robot SVG avatar to visually distinguish from human actors
- McpServer has access to KanbanContext through its board handle

## Subtasks

- [ ] Slugify name for actor ID
- [ ] Execute AddActor::agent with ensure, color, avatar
- [ ] Verify reconnection is idempotent
- [ ] Run `cargo test`

input.description:
Refactor the #tag CM6 infrastructure into a generic mention system parameterized by prefix character, then create @actor wrappers. Avoids duplicating 5 files.

## New files
- `ui/src/lib/mention-finder.ts` — `findMentionsInText(text, prefix, slugs)` returning `MentionHit[]`
- `ui/src/lib/cm-mention-decorations.ts` — generic ViewPlugin by prefix, CSS class, color facet
- `ui/src/lib/cm-mention-autocomplete.ts` — generic completion source by prefix
- `ui/src/lib/cm-mention-tooltip.ts` — generic hover tooltip by prefix
- `ui/src/lib/remark-mentions.ts` — generic remark AST transform by prefix

## Refactored files (preserve existing API as thin wrappers)
- `ui/src/lib/tag-finder.ts` → delegates to `mention-finder.ts` with prefix `#`
- `ui/src/lib/cm-tag-decorations.ts` → delegates to generic with prefix `#`
- `ui/src/lib/cm-tag-autocomplete.ts` → delegates to generic
- `ui/src/lib/cm-tag-tooltip.ts` → delegates to generic
- `ui/src/lib/remark-tags.ts` → delegates to generic

## Actor-specific exports
- `actorDecorations(colors)` — CSS class `cm-actor-pill`, slightly different styling
- `actorAutocomplete(actors)` — triggers on `@`, shows avatar in completion info, matches on name/id/actor_type
- `actorTooltips(meta)` — shows name, type badge, avatar

## Boundary rules
- Prefix char preceded by non-word char or SOL
- Slug followed by whitespace, `#`, `@`, or EOL
- Both `#tag` and `@actor` coexist in same text

## Subtasks
- [ ] Create generic mention-finder with tests
- [ ] Create generic cm-mention-decorations
- [ ] Create generic cm-mention-autocomplete
- [ ] Create generic cm-mention-tooltip
- [ ] Create generic remark-mentions
- [ ] Refactor tag files to delegate (preserve API)
- [ ] Create actor wrappers
- [ ] All existing tag tests pass unchanged
- [ ] Run `npm test`
input.title: Generic mention system (parameterize #tag into @actor)

input.description: Create the Avatar component and wire avatar display into the field dispatch system. ## New files - `ui/src/components/avatar.tsx` — low-level avatar renderer: data URI image or colored-initials circle - `ui/src/components/fields/displays/avatar-display.tsx` — renders overlapping avatar stack from actor ID array (for assignees field) ## Modified files - `ui/src/components/fields/displays/index.ts` — export AvatarDisplay, add `avatar` to resolveDisplay - `ui/src/components/cells/cell-dispatch.tsx` — add `avatar` case - `ui/src/components/entity-inspector.tsx` — add `avatar` to display dispatch ## Avatar component design - Props: `actorId: string`, `size?: "sm" | "md" | "lg"`, `className?` - Uses `useEntityStore().getEntity("actor", actorId)` to resolve actor - If actor has `avatar` field (data URI): render `` with rounded-full - Fallback: colored circle with initials (first letter of each word in name) - Color from actor's `color` field or deterministic hash ## AvatarDisplay design (for assignees reference field) - `value` is array of actor IDs - Renders row of overlapping `` components - Shows "+N" if more than 3 - Compact/full modes ## Subtasks - [ ] Create Avatar component with image/initials fallback - [ ] Create AvatarDisplay for actor ID arrays - [ ] Wire into resolveDisplay and CellDispatch - [ ] Wire into entity-inspector display dispatch - [ ] Run `npm test`

input.description:
Build a CM6-based multi-select editor for the `assignees` reference field. Uses @actor autocomplete with restricted input — only valid actor tokens allowed.

## New files
- `ui/src/components/fields/editors/multi-select-editor.tsx` — CM6 editor for reference fields

## Modified files
- `ui/src/components/fields/editors/index.ts` — export MultiSelectEditor, add `multi-select` to resolveEditor
- `ui/src/components/cells/cell-editor.tsx` — add `multi-select` case
- `ui/src/components/entity-inspector.tsx` — add `multi-select` to editor dispatch

## Design
- CM6 editor showing `@display-name` pills with autocomplete restricted to known actors
- **Storage**: field stores `["entity-id-1", "entity-id-2"]` (actor entity IDs)
- **Display in editor**: shows `@Alice @claude-code` as pills
- **Bidirectional mapping**: display name → entity ID (for commit), entity ID → display name (for render)
- **Autocomplete matching**: matches on actor `name`, raw `id`, and `actor_type` — typing `@cl` matches "claude-code", `@hu` matches all humans
- **Restricted mode**: free text rejected, only valid `@actor` tokens accepted. On commit, parse all `@slug` tokens, resolve to IDs, reject unknowns
- **Grid cell**: shows AvatarDisplay as popover trigger (no layout jump), popover contains CM6 editor
- **Inspector**: same component via editor dispatch
- Uses generic mention decorations/autocomplete from Card 5

## Subtasks
- [ ] Create MultiSelectEditor with CM6 + actor autocomplete
- [ ] Implement ID ↔ display-name bidirectional resolution
- [ ] Implement restricted mode (reject free text on commit)
- [ ] Wire into resolveEditor and CellEditor
- [ ] Wire into entity-inspector editor dispatch
- [ ] Grid: AvatarDisplay trigger + popover pattern (like color/date)
- [ ] Run `npm test`
input.title: CM6 multi-select editor for assignees field

Add actor entities to the frontend data loading pipeline and add a Tauri command for mention search.

## Changes
- `swissarmyhammer-kanban-app/ui/src/App.tsx` — add `actorEntities` state, fetch in `refresh()`, handle entity events
- `swissarmyhammer-kanban-app/src/commands.rs` — add `search_mentions(entity_type, query)` Tauri command that searches EntityContext by display name/id, returns matches with display_name, color, ID, avatar
- Frontend types — add mention metadata to schema types so entity type info flows to CM6

## Design
- In `refresh()`: add `invoke("list_entities", { entityType: "actor" })` to Promise.all
- Handle `entity-created/removed/field-changed` events for actor entity type
- `search_mentions` command: takes entity_type + query string, searches entities by their `mention_display_field`, returns `[{id, display_name, color, avatar}]`

- [ ] Handle actor entity events (create/remove/field-change)
- [ ] Add search_mentions Tauri command
- [ ] Expose mention metadata in frontend schema types

input.description:
Refactor the #tag CM6 infrastructure into a generic mention system parameterized by prefix character, with backend-driven autocomplete. Then create @actor wrappers.

## Architecture
- **Decorations/tooltips**: use a cached slug→color map pushed from backend via Tauri events (synchronous CM6 requirement)
- **Autocomplete**: async Tauri command `search_mentions(entity_type, query)` — CM6 natively supports async completion sources
- **Prefix→entity resolution**: driven by entity type metadata (`mention_prefix`, `mention_display_field`) from schema context

## New files
- `ui/src/lib/mention-finder.ts` — `findMentionsInText(text, prefix, slugs)` returning `MentionHit[]`
- `ui/src/lib/cm-mention-decorations.ts` — generic ViewPlugin parameterized by prefix, CSS class, color facet
- `ui/src/lib/cm-mention-autocomplete.ts` — generic async completion source: takes prefix + Tauri search function, debounces calls to backend
- `ui/src/lib/cm-mention-tooltip.ts` — generic hover tooltip by prefix
- `ui/src/lib/remark-mentions.ts` — generic remark AST transform by prefix

## Refactored files (preserve existing API as thin wrappers)
- `ui/src/lib/tag-finder.ts` → delegates to `mention-finder.ts` with prefix `#`
- `ui/src/lib/cm-tag-decorations.ts` → delegates to generic
- `ui/src/lib/cm-tag-autocomplete.ts` → delegates to generic (async via search_mentions)
- `ui/src/lib/cm-tag-tooltip.ts` → delegates to generic
- `ui/src/lib/remark-tags.ts` → delegates to generic

## Also: CM6 font fix
- `ui/src/lib/cm-keymap.ts` — add `fontFamily: "inherit"` to `.cm-content` in minimalTheme

## Subtasks
- [ ] Fix CM6 font: add fontFamily inherit to minimalTheme
- [ ] Create generic mention-finder with tests
- [ ] Create generic cm-mention-decorations (sync, cached map)
- [ ] Create generic cm-mention-autocomplete (async, Tauri backend)
- [ ] Create generic cm-mention-tooltip
- [ ] Create generic remark-mentions
- [ ] Refactor tag files to delegate (preserve API)
- [ ] Create actor wrappers
- [ ] All existing tag tests pass unchanged
- [ ] Run `npm test`

input.description:
Build a CM6-based multi-select editor for reference fields (assignees, depends_on, attachments). Uses mention autocomplete with restricted input.

## New files
- `ui/src/components/fields/editors/multi-select-editor.tsx` — CM6 editor for reference fields

## Modified files
- `ui/src/components/fields/editors/index.ts` — export MultiSelectEditor, add `multi-select` to resolveEditor
- `ui/src/components/cells/cell-editor.tsx` — add `multi-select` case
- `ui/src/components/entity-inspector.tsx` — add `multi-select` to editor dispatch

## Design
- Resolves mention behavior from the target entity type's metadata:
  - `field.type.entity = "actor"` → schema says actor has `mention_prefix: "@"`, `mention_display_field: "name"`
  - Editor automatically uses `@` prefix and searches actors by name
- CM6 shows `@Display-Name` pills with async autocomplete via `search_mentions` Tauri command
- **Autocomplete matching**: backend searches by display_name, id, and other indexed fields
- **Restricted mode**: on commit, parse all `@slug` tokens from CM6 content, resolve to entity IDs via cached map, reject unknown tokens
- **Storage**: commits array of entity IDs `["01ABC...", "01DEF..."]`
- **Bidirectional mapping**: entity ID → display name (for initial render), display name → entity ID (for commit)
- **Grid cell**: shows AvatarDisplay as popover trigger (no layout jump), popover contains CM6 editor
- **Inspector**: same component via editor dispatch
- Works generically for any reference field, not just assignees

## Subtasks
- [ ] Create MultiSelectEditor with CM6 + mention autocomplete
- [ ] Resolve mention prefix/display from target entity type metadata
- [ ] Implement ID ↔ display-name bidirectional resolution
- [ ] Implement restricted mode (reject free text on commit)
- [ ] Wire into resolveEditor and CellEditor
- [ ] Wire into entity-inspector editor dispatch
- [ ] Grid: AvatarDisplay trigger + popover pattern
- [ ] Run `npm test`

input.description:
Wire @actor (and all mentionable entity types) into EditableMarkdown and remark display.

## Key insight: markdown fields support ALL mentionable types
The body/description field doesn't know about specific entity types. Instead:
1. Schema context provides `getMentionableTypes()` → all entity types with `mention_prefix`
2. EditableMarkdown registers CM6 extensions for **each** mentionable type
3. Each prefix gets its own facet, completion source, and decorations — no collision
4. `#bug @alice` in body → tag + actor decorations, each resolved independently

## Changes
- `ui/src/components/editable-markdown.tsx` — replace hardcoded tag-only logic with generic loop over mentionable types from schema context. For each: register decorations (cached map), autocomplete (async search_mentions), tooltips.
- New `ui/src/components/actor-pill.tsx` — renders `@name` pill (or generic mention pill)
- `ui/src/components/entity-inspector.tsx` — no longer needs to manually pass tags/actors; EditableMarkdown reads mentionable types from schema context directly

## Subtasks
- [ ] EditableMarkdown reads mentionable types from schema context
- [ ] Loop over mentionable types to register CM6 extensions per prefix
- [ ] Create generic MentionPill component (replaces hardcoded TagPill for display)
- [ ] Update remark plugin to handle multiple prefixes
- [ ] Simplify inspector — remove manual tag/actor prop threading
- [ ] Run `npm test`

input.description:
Build a CM6-based multi-select editor for reference fields. Adapts behavior based on whether target entity type has a mention prefix.

## Two modes based on target entity type

**With mention prefix** (assignees → actor has `@`):
- CM6 shows `@Display-Name` pills with async autocomplete via search_mentions
- Restricted mode: only valid `@slug` tokens accepted, free text rejected
- On commit: parse `@slug` tokens → resolve to entity IDs

**Without mention prefix** (depends_on → task has no prefix):
- CM6 shows plain search input, autocomplete triggers on any text (no prefix char)
- Selected items render as plain pills (task title)
- On commit: resolve typed text to entity IDs

## How it discovers mention behavior
1. Read `field.type.entity` → e.g. `"actor"`
2. Look up entity type in schema context → `mention_prefix: "@"`, `mention_display_field: "name"`
3. If prefix exists: use prefix-based CM6 editing
4. If no prefix: use plain search-and-select mode

## Storage & resolution
- Stores array of entity IDs: `["01ABC...", "01DEF..."]`
- Bidirectional: ID → display name (render), display name → ID (commit)
- Grid cell: shows AvatarDisplay/BadgeList as popover trigger, popover has CM6 editor

## Files
- New `ui/src/components/fields/editors/multi-select-editor.tsx`
- Modified: `editors/index.ts`, `cell-editor.tsx`, `entity-inspector.tsx`

## Subtasks
- [ ] Create MultiSelectEditor with dual-mode (prefix vs plain search)
- [ ] Read target entity type's mention config from schema context
- [ ] Implement ID ↔ display-name resolution via cached maps
- [ ] Prefix mode: restricted @slug input with decorations
- [ ] Plain mode: search-and-select for entities without prefix
- [ ] Wire into resolveEditor, CellEditor, inspector
- [ ] Grid: display-as-trigger + popover pattern
- [ ] Run `npm test`

output.description:
Use `whoami` crate to automatically create an actor for the logged-in OS user when a board is opened.

## Changes
- `swissarmyhammer-kanban-app/Cargo.toml` — add `whoami` dependency
- `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/actor/add.rs` — extend `AddActor` to accept optional `color` and `avatar` fields
- `swissarmyhammer-kanban-app/src/state.rs` — add `ensure_os_actor()` called after `BoardHandle::open()`

## Design
- Actor ID = `whoami::username()`, display name = `whoami::realname()`
- Derive deterministic hex color from username hash (mod a curated palette)
- Generate initials-based SVG avatar as data URI
- Try to read macOS profile picture from filesystem, prefer real photo over generated
- Use `AddActor::human(username, realname).with_ensure()` — idempotent on re-open

## Subtasks
- [ ] Add `whoami` to Cargo.toml
- [ ] Extend AddActor with optional color/avatar fields
- [ ] Implement `ensure_os_actor()` in state.rs
- [ ] Generate deterministic color from username hash
- [ ] Generate initials SVG avatar (or read macOS profile picture)
- [ ] Call ensure_os_actor after BoardHandle::open()
- [ ] Run `cargo test`
output.title: Auto-create OS user actor on board open

output.description:
When an MCP client connects, auto-create an agent actor from the client info.

## Changes
- `swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/server.rs` — in `initialize()` handler (~line 920), after file watching setup, create actor

## Design
- Actor ID = slugified `request.client_info.name` (e.g. "claude-code")
- Display name = `request.client_info.name` (or `title` if present)
- `actor_type: agent`, `ensure: true`
- Derive deterministic color from name hash
- Generate geometric/robot SVG avatar to visually distinguish from human actors
- McpServer has access to KanbanContext through its board handle

## Subtasks
- [ ] Extract client_info.name in initialize() handler
- [ ] Slugify name for actor ID
- [ ] Execute AddActor::agent with ensure, color, avatar
- [ ] Verify reconnection is idempotent
- [ ] Run `cargo test`

Add actor entities to the frontend data loading pipeline and add a Tauri command for mention search.

## Changes
- `swissarmyhammer-kanban-app/ui/src/App.tsx` — add `actorEntities` state, fetch in `refresh()`, handle entity events
- `swissarmyhammer-kanban-app/src/commands.rs` — add `search_mentions(entity_type, query)` Tauri command that searches EntityContext by display name/id, returns matches with display_name, color, ID, avatar
- Frontend types — add mention metadata to schema types so entity type info flows to CM6

## Design
- In `refresh()`: add `invoke("list_entities", { entityType: "actor" })` to Promise.all
- Handle `entity-created/removed/field-changed` events for actor entity type
- `search_mentions` command: takes entity_type + query string, searches entities by their `mention_display_field`, returns `[{id, display_name, color, avatar}]`
- Schema context exposes entity type `mention_prefix` and `mention_display_field` to frontend

## Subtasks
- [ ] Add actorEntities state and loading
- [ ] Handle actor entity events (create/remove/field-change)
- [ ] Add search_mentions Tauri command
- [ ] Expose mention metadata in frontend schema types
- [ ] Run `npm test` and `cargo test`

output.description:
Refactor the #tag CM6 infrastructure into a generic mention system parameterized by prefix character, with backend-driven autocomplete. Then create @actor wrappers.

## Architecture
- **Decorations/tooltips**: use a cached slug→color map pushed from backend via Tauri events (synchronous CM6 requirement)
- **Autocomplete**: async Tauri command `search_mentions(entity_type, query)` — CM6 natively supports async completion sources
- **Prefix→entity resolution**: driven by entity type metadata (`mention_prefix`, `mention_display_field`) from schema context

## New files
- `ui/src/lib/mention-finder.ts` — `findMentionsInText(text, prefix, slugs)` returning `MentionHit[]`
- `ui/src/lib/cm-mention-decorations.ts` — generic ViewPlugin parameterized by prefix, CSS class, color facet
- `ui/src/lib/cm-mention-autocomplete.ts` — generic async completion source: takes prefix + Tauri search function, debounces calls to backend
- `ui/src/lib/cm-mention-tooltip.ts` — generic hover tooltip by prefix
- `ui/src/lib/remark-mentions.ts` — generic remark AST transform by prefix

## Refactored files (preserve existing API as thin wrappers)
- `ui/src/lib/tag-finder.ts` → delegates to `mention-finder.ts` with prefix `#`
- `ui/src/lib/cm-tag-decorations.ts` → delegates to generic
- `ui/src/lib/cm-tag-autocomplete.ts` → delegates to generic (async via search_mentions)
- `ui/src/lib/cm-tag-tooltip.ts` → delegates to generic
- `ui/src/lib/remark-tags.ts` → delegates to generic

## Also: CM6 font fix
- `ui/src/lib/cm-keymap.ts` — add `fontFamily: "inherit"` to `.cm-content` in minimalTheme

## Subtasks
- [ ] Fix CM6 font: add fontFamily inherit to minimalTheme
- [ ] Create generic mention-finder with tests
- [ ] Create generic cm-mention-decorations (sync, cached map)
- [ ] Create generic cm-mention-autocomplete (async, Tauri backend)
- [ ] Create generic cm-mention-tooltip
- [ ] Create generic remark-mentions
- [ ] Refactor tag files to delegate (preserve API)
- [ ] Create actor wrappers
- [ ] All existing tag tests pass unchanged
- [ ] Run `npm test`
output.title: Generic mention system (parameterize #tag into @actor)

output.description:
Wire @actor (and all mentionable entity types) into EditableMarkdown and remark display.

## Key insight: markdown fields support ALL mentionable types
The body/description field doesn't know about specific entity types. Instead:
1. Schema context provides `getMentionableTypes()` → all entity types with `mention_prefix`
2. EditableMarkdown registers CM6 extensions for **each** mentionable type
3. Each prefix gets its own facet, completion source, and decorations — no collision
4. `#bug @alice` in body → tag + actor decorations, each resolved independently

## Changes
- `ui/src/components/editable-markdown.tsx` — replace hardcoded tag-only logic with generic loop over mentionable types from schema context. For each: register decorations (cached map), autocomplete (async search_mentions), tooltips.
- New `ui/src/components/actor-pill.tsx` — renders `@name` pill (or generic mention pill)
- `ui/src/components/entity-inspector.tsx` — no longer needs to manually pass tags/actors; EditableMarkdown reads mentionable types from schema context directly

## Subtasks
- [ ] EditableMarkdown reads mentionable types from schema context
- [ ] Loop over mentionable types to register CM6 extensions per prefix
- [ ] Create generic MentionPill component (replaces hardcoded TagPill for display)
- [ ] Update remark plugin to handle multiple prefixes
- [ ] Simplify inspector — remove manual tag/actor prop threading
- [ ] Run `npm test`
output.title: Wire @actor into EditableMarkdown and remark display

output.description:
Build a CM6-based multi-select editor for reference fields. Adapts behavior based on whether target entity type has a mention prefix.

## Two modes based on target entity type

**With mention prefix** (assignees → actor has `@`):
- CM6 shows `@Display-Name` pills with async autocomplete via search_mentions
- Restricted mode: only valid `@slug` tokens accepted, free text rejected
- On commit: parse `@slug` tokens → resolve to entity IDs

**Without mention prefix** (depends_on → task has no prefix):
- CM6 shows plain search input, autocomplete triggers on any text (no prefix char)
- Selected items render as plain pills (task title)
- On commit: resolve typed text to entity IDs

## How it discovers mention behavior
1. Read `field.type.entity` → e.g. `"actor"`
2. Look up entity type in schema context → `mention_prefix: "@"`, `mention_display_field: "name"`
3. If prefix exists: use prefix-based CM6 editing
4. If no prefix: use plain search-and-select mode

## Storage & resolution
- Stores array of entity IDs: `["01ABC...", "01DEF..."]`
- Bidirectional: ID → display name (render), display name → ID (commit)
- Grid cell: shows AvatarDisplay/BadgeList as popover trigger, popover has CM6 editor

## Files
- New `ui/src/components/fields/editors/multi-select-editor.tsx`
- Modified: `editors/index.ts`, `cell-editor.tsx`, `entity-inspector.tsx`

## Subtasks
- [ ] Create MultiSelectEditor with dual-mode (prefix vs plain search)
- [ ] Read target entity type's mention config from schema context
- [ ] Implement ID ↔ display-name resolution via cached maps
- [ ] Prefix mode: restricted @slug input with decorations
- [ ] Plain mode: search-and-select for entities without prefix
- [ ] Wire into resolveEditor, CellEditor, inspector
- [ ] Grid: display-as-trigger + popover pattern
- [ ] Run `npm test`
output.title: CM6 multi-select editor for assignees field

input.description:
When clicking the + button on a column to add a new task, the task is created in the done column instead of the column where + was clicked. Additionally, only the 'todo' column should have the + button — it doesn't make sense to start cards in other columns.

Two fixes needed:
1. Fix task creation to respect the target column
2. Only show the + button on the first (todo) column

Key files: board-view.tsx (handleAddTask), column-view.tsx (+ button rendering), Rust dispatch for task.add

input.description: Must fix before merge
input.name: blocker

input.description:
**B1: Duplicate color palettes with divergent hash algorithms**

Three independent color derivation implementations exist:
- `state.rs:469` — djb2 hash over 18-color palette
- `avatar.tsx:38` — different hash function over different palette  
- `entity-inspector.tsx:283` — yet another palette inline

When an actor has no persisted `color` field, each location derives a different color from the same ID, causing visual mismatch between avatar, inspector, and backend default.

**Fix:** Extract a single canonical palette + hash into a shared location. Rust should persist the color on actor creation so the frontend never needs to derive it. Remove the frontend fallback palettes or align them as a safety net using the same algorithm.
input.title: Unify color palette and hash algorithm across Rust and TypeScript

output.description:
**B1: Duplicate color palettes with divergent hash algorithms**

Three independent color derivation implementations exist:
- `state.rs:469` — djb2 hash over 18-color palette
- `avatar.tsx:38` — different hash function over different palette  
- `entity-inspector.tsx:283` — yet another palette inline

When an actor has no persisted `color` field, each location derives a different color from the same ID, causing visual mismatch between avatar, inspector, and backend default.

**Fix:** Extract a single canonical palette + hash into a shared location. Rust should persist the color on actor creation so the frontend never needs to derive it. Remove the frontend fallback palettes or align them as a safety net using the same algorithm.
output.title: Unify color palette and hash algorithm across Rust and TypeScript

input.description:
**W1: `ensure` flag silently skips updates**

`state.rs` `ensure_os_actor` and MCP agent auto-create use `ensure: true`, which returns the existing actor without updating name/color/avatar. If the user changes their OS display name or profile picture, the stale actor persists forever.

**Fix:** When `ensure` finds an existing actor, compare and update changed fields (name, color, avatar) before returning.
input.title: ensure_actor should update existing actor fields

output.description:
**W1: `ensure` flag silently skips updates**

`state.rs` `ensure_os_actor` and MCP agent auto-create use `ensure: true`, which returns the existing actor without updating name/color/avatar. If the user changes their OS display name or profile picture, the stale actor persists forever.

**Fix:** When `ensure` finds an existing actor, compare and update changed fields (name, color, avatar) before returning.
output.title: ensure_actor should update existing actor fields

input.description:
**W2: Mention boundary detection uses hardcoded ASCII set**

`mention-finder.ts` uses a fixed set of boundary characters. Mentions adjacent to Unicode punctuation (e.g., CJK brackets, em-dashes) won't be detected.

**Fix:** Consider using a Unicode-aware word boundary regex or `\b` equivalent for mention detection boundaries.
input.title: Hardcoded mention boundary chars may miss Unicode

output.description:
**W2: Mention boundary detection uses hardcoded ASCII set**

`mention-finder.ts` uses a fixed set of boundary characters. Mentions adjacent to Unicode punctuation (e.g., CJK brackets, em-dashes) won't be detected.

**Fix:** Consider using a Unicode-aware word boundary regex or `\b` equivalent for mention detection boundaries.
output.title: Hardcoded mention boundary chars may miss Unicode

input.description:
**W3: No debounce on `search_mentions` Tauri invoke**

`cm-mention-autocomplete.ts` calls `invoke("search_mentions")` on every keystroke without debouncing. Fast typing generates redundant backend calls.

**Fix:** Add a ~150ms debounce or use CM6's built-in `activateOnTypingDelay` to throttle autocomplete invocations.
input.title: Add debounce to search_mentions in multi-select editor

output.description:
**W3: No debounce on `search_mentions` Tauri invoke**

`cm-mention-autocomplete.ts` calls `invoke("search_mentions")` on every keystroke without debouncing. Fast typing generates redundant backend calls.

**Fix:** Add a ~150ms debounce or use CM6's built-in `activateOnTypingDelay` to throttle autocomplete invocations.
output.title: Add debounce to search_mentions in multi-select editor

input.description:
**W4: Module-level Map caches grow unbounded**

Several mention-related modules use module-level `Map` objects as caches (slug→color, slug→entity). These are never cleared and grow with each new entity encountered.

**Fix:** Use WeakRef-based caches, add a size cap with LRU eviction, or tie cache lifetime to the EntityStore refresh cycle.
input.title: Module-level Map caches in mention system may leak

output.description:
**W4: Module-level Map caches grow unbounded**

Several mention-related modules use module-level `Map` objects as caches (slug→color, slug→entity). These are never cleared and grow with each new entity encountered.

**Fix:** Use WeakRef-based caches, add a size cap with LRU eviction, or tie cache lifetime to the EntityStore refresh cycle.
output.title: Module-level Map caches in mention system may leak

input.description:
**W5: Multi-select editor dropdown always visible**

The CM6 multi-select editor for reference fields (assignees, depends_on) renders the dropdown inline and always visible rather than as a popover triggered by clicking the cell display.

**Fix:** Wrap the CM6 editor in a Popover component, triggered by clicking the cell's avatar/pill display. The editor should auto-focus on open and close on blur or Escape.
input.title: Multi-select dropdown always visible instead of popover

output.description:
**W5: Multi-select editor dropdown always visible**

The CM6 multi-select editor for reference fields (assignees, depends_on) renders the dropdown inline and always visible rather than as a popover triggered by clicking the cell display.

**Fix:** Wrap the CM6 editor in a Popover component, triggered by clicking the cell's avatar/pill display. The editor should auto-focus on open and close on blur or Escape.
output.title: Multi-select dropdown always visible instead of popover

input.description:
**W6: + button restricted to first column without user-facing indication**

`board-view.tsx` passes `onAddTask` only to `i === 0`, so only the first column shows the + button. This is intentional per user request but may confuse other contributors.

**Fix:** Add a brief code comment explaining the UX rationale (tasks should start in the first workflow stage).
input.title: Document + button restriction to first column only

output.description:

- [ ] If the creating actor is an agent, add it to assignees automatically
- [ ] Add test for auto-assignment behavior

- MentionPill + Avatar for actor rendering

- [ ] Add tests for double-click inspect behavior

output.body:
FieldDef.id is bare `ulid::Ulid`. FieldsContext uses `HashMap<Ulid, usize>` for id_index. All methods that accept or return field IDs use bare Ulid. Need a FieldDefId newtype using define_id! macro.

~30 bare Ulid usages across types.rs, context.rs, compute.rs, validation.rs plus downstream in kanban defaults.rs.

- [x] Add `FieldDefId` to swissarmyhammer-fields/src/id_types.rs via define_id!
- [ ] Change FieldDef.id from Ulid to FieldDefId
- [ ] Update FieldsContext id_index and all methods
- [x] Update all test code constructing FieldDef
- [ ] Update kanban defaults.rs
- [ ] Re-export from lib.rs
- [ ] Run full test suite
output.title: Complete FieldDefId newtype — eliminate bare Ulid in fields crate

input.description:
Two broken editors in the grid:

1. **Tags field** (`kind: computed, editor: none`): `resolveEditor` falls through to markdown, giving a FieldPlaceholderEditor with no tag autocomplete. Typing `#test` shows "no word under cursor" and doesn't save (computed field).

Fix: Add `tag-select` editor type. CM6-based multi-select that:
- Shows current tags as pills
- CM6 autocomplete from available tags (via `search_mentions`)
- On select: calls `tag task` command (appends `#tag` to body)
- On remove: calls `untag task` command (removes `#tag` from body)
- `resolveEditor` returns `"tag-select"` for computed+parse-body-tags

2. **Assignees field** (`kind: reference, editor: multi-select`): Uses plain `<input type="text">` — MUST use CM6 per project rules.

Fix: Rewrite MultiSelectEditor to use CM6 with autocomplete.

- [ ] Create tag-select editor component using CM6
- [ ] Wire resolveEditor for computed parse-body-tags fields
- [ ] Rewrite MultiSelectEditor with CM6 instead of input
- [ ] Wire into CellEditor dispatch
- [ ] Test both editors in grid
input.title: Fix tag and assignee editors to use CM6

input.description: All 4 failures in src/lib/command-scope.test.tsx share the same root cause: `invoke("log_command", { cmd, target })` at command-scope.tsx:183 returns undefined in the test environment, so `.catch()` fails with TypeError. Tests affected: (1) executes a resolved command and returns true, (2) executes parent command when child does not register it, (3) handles async execute functions, (4) dispatchCommand > calls execute when set. Fix: mock `invoke` to return a Promise for the `log_command` call.
input.title: command-scope.test.tsx: invoke("log_command") returns undefined in test - 4 failures

output.description: All 4 failures in src/lib/command-scope.test.tsx share the same root cause: `invoke("log_command", { cmd, target })` at command-scope.tsx:183 returns undefined in the test environment, so `.catch()` fails with TypeError. Tests affected: (1) executes a resolved command and returns true, (2) executes parent command when child does not register it, (3) handles async execute functions, (4) dispatchCommand > calls execute when set. Fix: mock `invoke` to return a Promise for the `log_command` call.
output.title: command-scope.test.tsx: invoke("log_command") returns undefined in test - 4 failures

input.description: The test at line 236 in ui/src/components/fields/editors/multi-select-editor.test.tsx wraps all assertions inside `if (view) { ... }` where `getCmView()` returns null in jsdom. The entire assertion block is skipped and the test passes vacuously with zero assertions. The `getCmView` helper looks for `cmEditor.cmView.view` on the DOM element, which is not populated by @uiw/react-codemirror in jsdom. The test needs to either: (1) find another way to dispatch text into CM6 in jsdom, or (2) use `expect(view).toBeTruthy()` and fix the underlying issue so the view is accessible.
input.title: False positive: "resolves typed text" test in multi-select-editor.test.tsx - getCmView returns null in jsdom

input.description:
Implement the `parse-body-tags` derive handler in `swissarmyhammer-kanban` using the existing `tag_parser` module.

## Behavior

**compute**: Read `body_field` from entity, call `tag_parser::parse_tags()`, return tag slug array as `Value`.

**apply**: Read body, parse current tags, compare with desired array. For each tag to add: call `tag_parser::append_tag()` + auto-create tag entity if missing. For each tag to remove: call `tag_parser::remove_tag()`. Write updated body back.

The `apply` method reuses all existing `tag_parser` functions — no new parsing logic. Tag auto-creation reuses the existing pattern from `update_field.rs` (lines 94-114) and `TagTask`.

## Files
- New: `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/derive_handlers.rs` — `ParseBodyTags` struct implementing `DeriveHandler`
- Edit: `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/lib.rs` — export module
- Edit: `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/context.rs` — register handler in `DeriveRegistry` during board open

## Checklist
- [ ] Implement `ParseBodyTags` struct with `compute` and `apply`
- [ ] `compute` reads body_field, calls parse_tags, returns JSON array of slugs
- [ ] `apply` diffs current vs desired, calls append_tag/remove_tag on body text
- [ ] `apply` auto-creates tag entities for new tags (reuse existing auto_color pattern)
- [ ] Register `"parse-body-tags"` handler in KanbanContext
- [ ] Unit test: compute returns correct tags from body
- [ ] Unit test: apply adds new tag to body
- [ ] Unit test: apply removes tag from body
- [ ] Unit test: apply auto-creates tag entity for unknown tag
- [ ] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-kanban` passes
input.title: Implement ParseBodyTags derive handler

input.description:
Make `entity/update_field.rs` smart about computed fields. When the target field has `kind: computed`, look up its derive rule in the `DeriveRegistry` and call `apply()` instead of doing a direct field write.

## Current behavior
- `update_field` validates field name against entity schema
- Reads entity, sets field value, writes entity
- Has hardcoded tag auto-creation for body field updates (lines 94-114)

## New behavior
- Look up `FieldDef` for the target field via `FieldsContext::get_field_by_name()`
- If `FieldType::Computed { derive }`: look up handler in `DeriveRegistry`, call `handler.apply(entity, schema, value)`. If handler doesn't exist or `writable() == false`, return error.
- If normal field: existing direct-write path (unchanged)
- Remove the hardcoded tag auto-creation block (lines 94-114) — this is now handled by the `ParseBodyTags::apply()` handler

## Key: DeriveRegistry access
The `DeriveRegistry` needs to be accessible from `UpdateEntityField::execute()`. Options:
- Store in `KanbanContext` (parallel to `FieldsContext`)
- Store in `EntityContext` (next to `FieldsContext`)

`KanbanContext` is the right place since derive handlers are kanban-domain specific (tag_parser, auto_color), not generic entity operations.

## Files
- Edit: `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/entity/update_field.rs` — add computed field routing
- Edit: `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/context.rs` — expose `DeriveRegistry` from `KanbanContext`

## Checklist
- [ ] Add `derive_registry` field to `KanbanContext`
- [ ] In `UpdateEntityField::execute()`, check if field is computed
- [ ] If computed: look up handler, call `apply()`, write entity
- [ ] If normal: existing direct write (unchanged)
- [ ] Remove hardcoded tag auto-creation from body field update path
- [ ] Existing tests still pass (update_body_auto_creates_tag_entities now goes through DeriveHandler)
- [ ] New test: `update_field` on computed "tags" field calls derive handler
- [ ] New test: `update_field` on computed field with no handler returns error
- [ ] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-kanban` passes
input.title: UpdateEntityField routes computed fields through DeriveHandler

input.description:
Wire up the missing `task.tag` command registration and change the tags field editor from `none` to `multi-select` so the frontend can edit computed tag fields through the standard editor path.

## task.tag registration
`TagTask` exists in `task/tag.rs` but is NOT registered in `commands/mod.rs`. Add a `TagTaskCmd` wrapper (like `UntagTaskCmd`) and register it as `"task.tag"`.

The `TagTaskCmd` should accept args `id` (task) and `tag` (tag name) — same as the existing MCP kanban tool's `tag task` operation.

## Tags field editor
Currently `builtin/fields/definitions/tags.yaml` has `editor: none` because computed fields were read-only. Now that `update_field` routes through `DeriveHandler`, change to `editor: multi-select` so the frontend knows this field is editable.

## Files
- Edit: `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/commands/task_commands.rs` — add `TagTaskCmd`
- Edit: `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/commands/mod.rs` — register `"task.tag"`
- Edit: `swissarmyhammer-kanban/builtin/fields/definitions/tags.yaml` — change `editor: none` to `editor: multi-select`

## Checklist
- [ ] Add `TagTaskCmd` struct in task_commands.rs
- [ ] Register `"task.tag"` in `register_commands()`
- [ ] Update tags.yaml editor from `none` to `multi-select`
- [ ] Test: `task.tag` command is available and executes
- [ ] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-kanban` passes
input.title: Register task.tag command and update tags field editor to multi-select

input.description:
Now that the backend handles computed field writes via `entity.update_field`, remove all the frontend body-manipulation hacks from `multi-select-editor.tsx`.

## Current state
The multi-select editor has two code paths:
1. **Reference fields** (assignees): commits array of IDs via `onCommit(selectedIds)` — works correctly
2. **Computed tags** (`isComputedTags`): reads task body, does string manipulation (`#tag` append/remove), calls `entity.update_field` on the body — THIS IS THE HACK

## New state
Both paths commit the same way: `onCommit(selectedIds)`. The grid's `handleCommit` calls `updateField(entity_type, id, field_name, value)` which sends `entity.update_field` to the backend. The backend sees the field is computed, routes through `DeriveHandler`, and handles body mutation server-side.

## Changes
- Remove `isComputedTags` branching in `addItem`, `removeItem`, and `commit`
- Remove direct `invoke("dispatch_command", ...)` calls for body mutation
- Remove `getStr(entity, "body")` usage
- `addItem` just adds to `selectedIds` (same as reference path)
- `removeItem` just removes from `selectedIds` (same as reference path)
- `commit` always calls `onCommit(selectedIds)` (never `onCancel`)
- Tags field now uses `editor: multi-select` from schema, so frontend auto-opens the multi-select editor

## Files
- Edit: `ui/src/components/fields/editors/multi-select-editor.tsx` — simplify to single code path
- Edit: `ui/src/components/fields/editors/multi-select-editor.test.tsx` — update tag tests (no longer mocking invoke for body updates)
- May need: `ui/src/lib/schema-context.tsx` — ensure computed fields with `editor: multi-select` are treated as editable

## Checklist
- [ ] Remove `isComputedTags` branches from addItem, removeItem, commit
- [ ] Both reference and computed fields use identical commit path
- [ ] Remove direct invoke calls for body field manipulation
- [ ] Update tests: tag field commits array via onCommit, not via invoke
- [ ] Verify assignee editing still works (no regression)
- [ ] `npx vitest run` passes
- [ ] `npx tsc --noEmit` passes
input.title: Frontend: remove body string manipulation from MultiSelectEditor

The `TagTaskCmd` should accept args `id` (task) and `tag` (tag name) — same as the existing MCP kanban tool's `tag task` operation.

## Tags field editor
Currently `builtin/fields/definitions/tags.yaml` has `editor: none` because computed fields were read-only. Now that `update_field` routes through `DeriveHandler`, change to `editor: multi-select` so the frontend knows this field is editable.

- Edit: `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/commands/mod.rs` — register `"task.tag"`
- Edit: `swissarmyhammer-kanban/builtin/fields/definitions/tags.yaml` — change `editor: none` to `editor: multi-select`

output.description:
Now that the backend handles computed field writes via `entity.update_field`, remove all the frontend body-manipulation hacks from `multi-select-editor.tsx`.

## Current state
The multi-select editor has two code paths:
1. **Reference fields** (assignees): commits array of IDs via `onCommit(selectedIds)` — works correctly
2. **Computed tags** (`isComputedTags`): reads task body, does string manipulation (`#tag` append/remove), calls `entity.update_field` on the body — THIS IS THE HACK

## New state
Both paths commit the same way: `onCommit(selectedIds)`. The grid's `handleCommit` calls `updateField(entity_type, id, field_name, value)` which sends `entity.update_field` to the backend. The backend sees the field is computed, routes through `DeriveHandler`, and handles body mutation server-side.

## Changes
- Remove `isComputedTags` branching in `addItem`, `removeItem`, and `commit`
- Remove direct `invoke("dispatch_command", ...)` calls for body mutation
- Remove `getStr(entity, "body")` usage
- `addItem` just adds to `selectedIds` (same as reference path)
- `removeItem` just removes from `selectedIds` (same as reference path)
- `commit` always calls `onCommit(selectedIds)` (never `onCancel`)
- Tags field now uses `editor: multi-select` from schema, so frontend auto-opens the multi-select editor

## Files
- Edit: `ui/src/components/fields/editors/multi-select-editor.tsx` — simplify to single code path
- Edit: `ui/src/components/fields/editors/multi-select-editor.test.tsx` — update tag tests (no longer mocking invoke for body updates)
- May need: `ui/src/lib/schema-context.tsx` — ensure computed fields with `editor: multi-select` are treated as editable

## Checklist
- [ ] Remove `isComputedTags` branches from addItem, removeItem, commit
- [ ] Both reference and computed fields use identical commit path
- [ ] Remove direct invoke calls for body field manipulation
- [ ] Update tests: tag field commits array via onCommit, not via invoke
- [ ] Verify assignee editing still works (no regression)
- [ ] `npx vitest run` passes
- [ ] `npx tsc --noEmit` passes
output.title: Frontend: remove body string manipulation from MultiSelectEditor

output.description:
Implement the `parse-body-tags` derive handler in `swissarmyhammer-kanban` using the existing `tag_parser` module.

## Behavior

**compute**: Read `body_field` from entity, call `tag_parser::parse_tags()`, return tag slug array as `Value`.

**apply**: Read body, parse current tags, compare with desired array. For each tag to add: call `tag_parser::append_tag()` + auto-create tag entity if missing. For each tag to remove: call `tag_parser::remove_tag()`. Write updated body back.

The `apply` method reuses all existing `tag_parser` functions — no new parsing logic. Tag auto-creation reuses the existing pattern from `update_field.rs` (lines 94-114) and `TagTask`.

## Files
- New: `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/derive_handlers.rs` — `ParseBodyTags` struct implementing `DeriveHandler`
- Edit: `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/lib.rs` — export module
- Edit: `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/context.rs` — register handler in `DeriveRegistry` during board open

## Checklist
- [ ] Implement `ParseBodyTags` struct with `compute` and `apply`
- [ ] `compute` reads body_field, calls parse_tags, returns JSON array of slugs
- [ ] `apply` diffs current vs desired, calls append_tag/remove_tag on body text
- [ ] `apply` auto-creates tag entities for new tags (reuse existing auto_color pattern)
- [ ] Register `"parse-body-tags"` handler in KanbanContext
- [ ] Unit test: compute returns correct tags from body
- [ ] Unit test: apply adds new tag to body
- [ ] Unit test: apply removes tag from body
- [ ] Unit test: apply auto-creates tag entity for unknown tag
- [ ] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-kanban` passes

output.description:
Make `entity/update_field.rs` smart about computed fields. When the target field has `kind: computed`, look up its derive rule in the `DeriveRegistry` and call `apply()` instead of doing a direct field write.

## Current behavior
- `update_field` validates field name against entity schema
- Reads entity, sets field value, writes entity
- Has hardcoded tag auto-creation for body field updates (lines 94-114)

## New behavior
- Look up `FieldDef` for the target field via `FieldsContext::get_field_by_name()`
- If `FieldType::Computed { derive }`: look up handler in `DeriveRegistry`, call `handler.apply(entity, schema, value)`. If handler doesn't exist or `writable() == false`, return error.
- If normal field: existing direct-write path (unchanged)
- Remove the hardcoded tag auto-creation block (lines 94-114) — this is now handled by the `ParseBodyTags::apply()` handler

## Key: DeriveRegistry access
The `DeriveRegistry` needs to be accessible from `UpdateEntityField::execute()`. Options:
- Store in `KanbanContext` (parallel to `FieldsContext`)
- Store in `EntityContext` (next to `FieldsContext`)

`KanbanContext` is the right place since derive handlers are kanban-domain specific (tag_parser, auto_color), not generic entity operations.

## Files
- Edit: `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/entity/update_field.rs` — add computed field routing
- Edit: `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/context.rs` — expose `DeriveRegistry` from `KanbanContext`

## Checklist
- [ ] Add `derive_registry` field to `KanbanContext`
- [ ] In `UpdateEntityField::execute()`, check if field is computed
- [ ] If computed: look up handler, call `apply()`, write entity
- [ ] If normal: existing direct write (unchanged)
- [ ] Remove hardcoded tag auto-creation from body field update path
- [ ] Existing tests still pass (update_body_auto_creates_tag_entities now goes through DeriveHandler)
- [ ] New test: `update_field` on computed "tags" field calls derive handler
- [ ] New test: `update_field` on computed field with no handler returns error
- [ ] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-kanban` passes
output.title: UpdateEntityField routes computed fields through DeriveHandler
